title: elaeocarpus sepikanus
intro: Elaeocarpus sepikanus is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, commonly known as the mountain apple or blue berry tree. It is native to the tropical forests of Papua New Guinea and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus sepikanus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide relief from various ailments.
